OCR Evaluation — book_cover

[OCR] Extract pick_orientation(); fix annotation using wrong orientation code 90bb422586b7e80c6fe6531e69a2adea8d0b86ea _annotate_image_b64 was calling correct_orientation() (variance only) while recognize() uses the OCR-confidence tiebreaker for 90°/270° ambiguity. For IMG_4256 this caused the annotation to rotate to 90° while OCR used 270°, so bboxes landed in the wrong position on the displayed image. Fix: extract pick_orientation() from recognize() into a public function that includes the tiebreaker, then use it in both recognize() and _annotate_image_b64. Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com> eval_20260628_002  ·  2026-06-28 01:03:45.550721 UTC
NEEDS IMPROVEMENT 304.1% mean CER  (threshold < 30%)

Failed: IMG_4259.JPG (recall 0%), IMG_4260.JPG (recall 50%). Passed: IMG_4258.JPG (recall 100%). Failure mode(s): key phrases missing from output (IMG_4259.JPG, IMG_4260.JPG).

Character Error Rate (CER) — edit distance between recognised and expected text, divided by the length of the expected text. Lower is better; 0% = perfect match.
< 10% excellent 10–30% acceptable 30–60% poor > 60% failing > 100% hypothesis longer than reference
